title: Vietnamese Shaking Beef (Bo Luc Lac)
description: Experience authentic Vietnamese flavors with this sizzling beef dish featuring perfectly seared sirloin cubes tossed in a savory marinade and served over fresh watercress with pickled onions and zesty lime dipping sauce. The high-heat searing technique creates an irresistible caramelized crust while keeping the beef tender inside.
ingredients: 1 1/2 lbs beef top sirloin, fat trimmed, cut into 1 inch cubes
1 tsp thick soy sauce (dark soy sauce)
4 cups watercress leaves or mixed baby greens
instructions: Marinate the beef by combining 5 cloves crushed garlic, 1 tbsp oyster sauce, 1 tbsp agave (or sugar), 1 tbsp fish sauce, 1 tsp thick soy sauce, and 1 tsp sesame oil in a bowl. Add 1 1/2 lbs beef cubes and toss to coat. Cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour, or overnight for best results.
Make the vinaigrette by whisking together 1/2 cup rice vinegar, 1 1/2 tbsp sugar, and 1 1/2 tsp kosher salt until dissolved. Taste and adjust for balance of sour, salty, and sweet.

Pickle the onions by placing thinly sliced red onion in a small bowl. Pour about 1/4 cup of the vinaigrette over them, cover, and refrigerate for at least 10 minutes. Reserve the remaining vinaigrette separately.
Prepare the platter by arranging 4 cups watercress and 2 sliced tomatoes on a serving platter. Set aside.
Make the dipping sauce by combining juice of 1 lime with 1/2 tsp kosher salt and 1/2 tsp cracked black pepper in a small ramekin. Stir to dissolve.

Sear the first batch of beef by heating a large wok or heavy skillet over high heat for 1-2 minutes until very hot. Add 1/2 tbsp canola oil. When oil just begins to smoke, add half the marinated beef in a single layer. Do not move the beef for a full 2 minutes to develop proper sear. Shake the pan or flip with tongs to sear opposite sides for 1-2 minutes more. Transfer to a plate.

Sear the second batch by returning the wok to high heat, adding remaining 1/2 tbsp oil, and repeating with second half of beef. Do not overcrowd the pan as this is essential for searing rather than steaming.

Assemble by transferring all seared beef onto the bed of watercress and tomatoes. Drizzle remaining vinaigrette over beef and greens. Top with pickled red onions (drained). Serve immediately with lime dipping sauce on the side.
notes: Thick soy sauce equals dark soy sauce (not regular soy sauce). If unavailable, substitute with 1/2 tsp regular soy sauce plus 1/2 tsp molasses.
Pat the beef dry before searing if marinade is very wet - excess moisture prevents browning.
Use wok or cast iron skillet - nonstick pans won't get hot enough for proper sear.
For gluten-free: use tamari instead of thick soy sauce and ensure oyster sauce is gluten-free certified.
Bring marinated beef to room temperature for 20 minutes before cooking for more even results.
Cook beef to medium or medium-rare inside. Total cook time per batch is 3-4 minutes. Do not overcook as sirloin becomes tough quickly.
